questionHtml:
A, B and C started a business in partnership Initially, A invested ₹29,000, while B and C invested ₹25,000 each. After 4 months, A withdrew ₹3,000. After 2 more months, C invested ₹12,000 more. Find the share of C (in ₹) in the profit of ₹33,200 at the end of the year.

  1. 10,800

  2. 10,000

  3. 11,067

  4. 12,400
subject: Quant

_source.testSeries: Math [P.Y.P] Chapter wise test SSC CGL 2021 (11-04-2022 to 21-04-2022 )
_source.product: Chapter Wise Tests for All SSC Exams : 2026 (EDUQUITY BASED) Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
The investments of three partners, A, B, and C, are initially in the ratio 5 : 6 : 7. After a year, A adds ₹40,000, B adds ₹35,000, and C adds some amount to their respective investments. The new ratio of their investments becomes 7 : 8 : 9. Find the amount (in ₹) added by partner C.

  1. ₹20,000

  2. ₹25000

  3. ₹30,000

  4. ₹36,000
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
Amit invested ₹90,000 in a business. After 3 months, Sumit joined the partnership with ₹60,000. After another 3 months, Amit withdrew ₹30,000. Find the ratio of their profits at the end of the year.

  1. 3 : 2

  2. 4 : 3

  3. 5 : 3

  4. 2 : 1
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

_source.testSeries: SSC CGL 2025 Chapter wise Quantitative Aptitude
_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
Ram and Mohan invest ₹70,000 and ₹50,000 respectively. After 1 year, the total profit is distributed including simple interest at 10% per annum on the capital of each partner. If the total profit including interest is ₹18,000, what is Ram's share of the profit ?

  1. ₹7,000

  2. ₹10,500

  3. ₹8,000

  4. ₹8,500
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
P and Q start a shop with a capital of ₹1,50,000 and ₹4,50,000, respectively. After a year, out of the profit of ₹1,60,000, P gets his share of profit plus some money that is not a part of the profit, as his salary. If P gets a total of ₹70,000, what is the salary (in ₹) he received?

  1. 40,000

  2. 30,000

  3. 25,000

  4. 50,000
subject: Quant

_source.testSeries: Math [P.Y.P] Chapter wise test SSC CGL 2021 (11-04-2022 to 21-04-2022 )
_source.product: Chapter Wise Tests for All SSC Exams : 2026 (EDUQUITY BASED) Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
P and Q contribute to a business investment in the ratio 3 : 5. They earn a profit of ₹66,000. P is a working partner and receives 10% of the profit extra. What is P's total share?

  1. ₹26,500

  2. ₹28,875

  3. ₹38,875

  4. ₹36,875
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

_source.testSeries: SSC CGL 2025 Chapter wise Quantitative Aptitude
_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
P and Q start a business with Rs. 2,40,000 and Rs. 3,60,000 respectively. After 4 months, P adds Rs. 60,000 more, and Q withdraws Rs.60,000. At the end of 1 year, they make a profit of Rs. 2,40,000. What is Q's share of profit ?

  1. Rs.1,28,000

  2. Rs.1,25,000

  3. Rs. 2,00,000

  4. Rs. 2,90,000
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
P, Q, and R started a textile business. Their initial capital investments were ₹1,200,000, ₹800,000, and ₹1,000,000, respectively. After four months, P added ₹200,000, and R withdrew ₹100,000. Q kept his investment unchanged. The partnership lasted for 12 months (one year). What was the ratio of their effective capital contributions to profits ?

  1. 10 : 4 : 15

  2. 16 : 15 : 7

  3. 10 : 7 : 5

  4. 10 : 6 : 7
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
A and B entered into a partnership with certain investment At the end of 8 months A withdrew and collected back his money. A and B received profit in the ratio of 5:9 at the end of the year. If B had invested ₹36,000 then how much capital A had invested(in ₹)

  1. 25,000

  2. 30,000

  3. 20,000

  4. 36,000
subject: Quant

_source.testSeries: Math [P.Y.P] Chapter wise test SSC CGL 2021 (11-04-2022 to 21-04-2022 )
_source.product: Chapter Wise Tests for All SSC Exams : 2026 (EDUQUITY BASED) Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
In a three-way joint venture, the first investor contributes capital, the second contributes time and strategy, and the third brings a network of suppliers. They agree to share profits in the ratio 5 : 6 : 3. If the venture earns Rs. 2,80,000 in the first year, how much should the strategist (second partner) receive ?

  1. Rs.140,000

  2. Rs.165,000

  3. Rs.120,000

  4. Rs.155,000
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
A is a working partner and B is a sleeping partner in a business. A invested ₹30,000 and B invested ₹50,000. A receives 20% of the profit for managing the business and the rest is divided in proportion to their capital. What does B get out of the profit of ₹10,000 ?

  1. ₹5,500

  2. ₹5,000

  3. ₹4,500

  4. ₹4,000
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC

questionHtml:
10. Keshav, Surjeet and Thomas started a business with investments in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. The ratio of their period of investments is 5 : 6 : 9. Twenty percent of the profit was spent on rent and maintenance of the office. Remaining profit was distributed among themselves. If the difference in the share of profit of Keshav and Surjeet is Rs.7264 then how much is the total profit (in Rs.)?

 

  1. 51060

  2. 58112

  3. 46490

  4. 72640
subject: Quant

_source.testSeries: Math [P.Y.P] Chapter wise test SSC CGL 2020 (13-08-2021 to 24-08-2021 )

  3. 8

  4. 6

questionHtml:
If two partners invest for the same period but share profits in 3:5, and the total profit is Rs. 24,000, what is the capital of the second partner if the first invested Rs. 12,000?

  1. Rs. 15,000

  2. Rs. 20,000

  3. Rs. 25,000

  4. Rs. 18,000

questionHtml:
In a partnership firm two partners, Vijay and Praveen are working on an assignment. It will take 12 weeks for Vijay to complete the entire assignment alone, while Praveen will take 8 weeks to complete it alone. Due to work pressure, they decided to work on that assignment on the alternate week basis. In the first week Praveen will work and in the second week Vijay will work and so on. In how many weeks the work will be completed if they work on alternate week basis?

  1. 8

  2. 8.5

  3. 9.5

  4. 9
subject: Quant

_source.product: SSC Eduquity Test Pass : 2026 Exam: SSC
